perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, SAP and Company, intending to be legally bound hereby, agree to the following: 1. In connection with an evaluation relating to a potential relationship, cooperation or transaction (the “Evaluation”), SAP and Company may deliver to each other, upon the execution of this Agreement, Confidential Information as defined below (the party disclosing such Confidential Information being the “Disclosing Party” and the party receiving such Confidential Information being the “Receiving Party”).
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, SAP and Company, intending to be legally bound hereby, agree to the following: 1. In connection with an evaluation relating to a potential relationship, cooperation or transaction (the “Assessment”), SAP and Company may deliver to each other, upon the execution of this Agreement, Confidential Information as defined below (the party disclosing such Confidential Information being the “Information Provider” and the party receiving such Confidential Information being the “Information Recipient”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Evaluation" to "Assessment", "Disclosing Party" to "Information Provider" and "Receiving Party" to "Information Recipient" introduces ambiguity. Later sections rely on the original definitions of "Evaluation", "Disclosing Party", and "Receiving Party." This creates an in-text contradiction as later clauses may not be clearly applicable with the new terms. For example, Section 3 refers to "Disclosing Party" and "Receiving Party" which now creates confusion on what party is being refered to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party: (a) shall take all reasonable steps (defined below) to keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential; (b) shall not disclose or reveal any Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Evaluation; (c) shall not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with the Evaluation; and (d) shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of the Evaluation) any information about the Evaluation, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives. As used herein “reasonable steps” means those steps the Receiving Party takes to protect its own similar proprietary and confidential information, which shall not be less than a reasonable standard of care. As used herein, “Representatives” shall mean (i) employees of the Receiving Party; (ii) attorneys, accountants, or other professional business advisors; and, additionally, (iii) employees of those entities directly or indirectly owned by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives. Each party shall promptly advise the other party of any misuse of Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Evaluation" with "Assessment", "Disclosing Party" with "Information Provider", and "Receiving Party" with "Information Recipient". The contradicted text refers to "Receiving Party" and "Evaluation". This creates a direct contradiction because the document now uses different terms to refer to the same entity and process without defining them as interchangeable. This misalignment of terminology, as described in the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, creates ambiguity and a strong contradiction. The contradicted clause now uses terms that have been replaced elsewhere in the document.

perturbation.original_text: As used herein, “Confidential Information” shall mean all information furnished by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives (defined below) to the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is either designated in writing by the Disclosing Party as confidential or should be reasonably understood by the Receiving Party to be confidential, including but not limited to, information that is related to: (a) the business plans or operations of the Disclosing Party; (b) the research and development or investigations of the Disclosing Party; (c) the business of any customer or partner of the Disclosing Party; (d) the Disclosing Party’s properties, employees, customers, finances, operations; (e) any information about or concerning any third party (which information was provided to the Disclosing Party subject to an applicable confidentiality obligation to such third party); (f) software and related documentation (“Disclosing Party’s Software”) including but not limited to the following information regarding the Disclosing Party’s Software: (i) computer software (object and source codes), programming techniques and programming concepts, methods of processing, system designs embodied in the Disclosing Party’s Software; and (ii) discoveries, inventions, concepts, designs, flow charts, documentation, product specifications, application program interface specifications, techniques and processes relating to the Disclosing Party’s Software; and (g) product offerings, content partners, product pricing, product availability, technical drawings, algorithms, processes, ideas, techniques, formulas, data, schematics, trade secrets, know-how, improvements, inventions (whether patentable or not), marketing plans, forecasts and strategies.
perturbation.changed_text: As used herein, “Sensitive Data” shall mean all information furnished by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives (defined below) to the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is either designated in writing by the Disclosing Party as confidential or should be reasonably understood by the Receiving Party to be confidential, including but not limited to, information that is related to: (a) the business plans or operations of the Disclosing Party; (b) the research and development or investigations of the Disclosing Party; (c) the business of any customer or partner of the Disclosing Party; (d) the Disclosing Party’s properties, employees, customers, finances, operations; (e) any information about or concerning any third party (which information was provided to the Disclosing Party subject to an applicable confidentiality obligation to such third party); (f) software and related documentation (“Disclosing Party’s Software”) including but not limited to the following information regarding the Disclosing Party’s Software: (i) computer software (object and source codes), programming techniques and programming concepts, methods of processing, system designs embodied in the Disclosing Party’s Software; and (ii) discoveries, inventions, concepts, designs, flow charts, documentation, product specifications, application program interface specifications, techniques and processes relating to the Disclosing Party’s Software; and (g) product offerings, content partners, product pricing, product availability, technical drawings, algorithms, processes, ideas, techniques, formulas, data, schematics, trade secrets, know-how, improvements, inventions (whether patentable or not), marketing plans, forecasts and strategies.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Data" without updating subsequent references to "Confidential Information" creates an in-text contradiction. For example, section 3 still refers to "Confidential Information". This creates ambiguity as to whether the obligations in those sections apply to "Sensitive Data".
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party: (a) shall take all reasonable steps (defined below) to keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential; (b) shall not disclose or reveal any Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Evaluation; (c) shall not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with the Evaluation; and (d) shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of the Evaluation) any information about the Evaluation, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives. As used herein “reasonable steps” means those steps the Receiving Party takes to protect its own similar proprietary and confidential information, which shall not be less than a reasonable standard of care. As used herein, “Representatives” shall mean (i) employees of the Receiving Party; (ii) attorneys, accountants, or other professional business advisors; and, additionally, (iii) employees of those entities directly or indirectly owned by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives. Each party shall promptly advise the other party of any misuse of Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Data" without updating the contradicted text, which still uses "Confidential Information," creates a clear in-text contradiction under the definition of Misaligned Terminology. The contradicted text defines obligations related to "Confidential Information," but the modified text now defines "Sensitive Data." This creates ambiguity and inconsistency because it is unclear if the obligations in the contradicted text apply to "Sensitive Data." A reasonable reader would immediately recognize the sections as incompatible since the obligations regarding 'Confidential Information' will not apply to the newly defined 'Sensitive Data' per the change, thus a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not be reproduced in any form except as required to accomplish the intent of this Agreement. Any reproduction of any Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall contain any and all confidential or proprietary notices or legends which appear on the original. The Receiving Party: (a) shall take all reasonable steps (defined below) to keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential; (b) shall not disclose or reveal any Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Evaluation; (c) shall not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with the Evaluation; and (d) shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of the Evaluation) any information about the Evaluation, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives. As used herein “reasonable steps” means those steps the Receiving Party takes to protect its own similar proprietary and confidential information, which shall not be less than a reasonable standard of care. As used herein, “Representatives” shall mean (i) employees of the Receiving Party; (ii) attorneys, accountants, or other professional business advisors; and, additionally, (iii) employees of those entities directly or indirectly owned by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives. Each party shall promptly advise the other party of any misuse of Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Data shall not be reproduced in any form except as required to accomplish the intent of this Agreement. Any reproduction of any Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall contain any and all confidential or proprietary notices or legends which appear on the original. The Receiving Party: (a) shall take all reasonable steps (defined below) to keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential; (b) shall not disclose or reveal any Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Evaluation; (c) shall not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with the Evaluation; and (d) shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of the Evaluation) any information about the Evaluation, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives. As used herein “reasonable steps” means those steps the Receiving Party takes to protect its own similar proprietary and confidential information, which shall not be less than a reasonable standard of care. As used herein, “Representatives” shall mean (i) employees of the Receiving Party; (ii) attorneys, accountants, or other professional business advisors; and, additionally, (iii) employees of those entities directly or indirectly owned by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives. Each party shall promptly advise the other party of any misuse of Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the first sentence to define the information as "Proprietary Data" while the rest of the section refers to "Confidential Information" creates contradiction within the same section. It's now unclear which set of information the obligations apply to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall not be reproduced in any form except as required to accomplish the intent of this Agreement. Any reproduction of any Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall contain any and all confidential or proprietary notices or legends which appear on the original. The Receiving Party: (a) shall take all reasonable steps (defined below) to keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential; (b) shall not disclose or reveal any Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Evaluation; (c) shall not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with the Evaluation; and (d) shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of the Evaluation) any information about the Evaluation, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives. As used herein “reasonable steps” means those steps the Receiving Party takes to protect its own similar proprietary and confidential information, which shall not be less than a reasonable standard of care. As used herein, “Representatives” shall mean (i) employees of the Receiving Party; (ii) attorneys, accountants, or other professional business advisors; and, additionally, (iii) employees of those entities directly or indirectly owned by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives. Each party shall promptly advise the other party of any misuse of Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data' in the first sentence of a section that repeatedly refers to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a misalignment of terminology, as described in the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type. The contradicted text is the same section of text as the changed text; however, the change to the Changed Text, when compared to the Original Text, causes the contradiction. The contradiction is that the section becomes unclear as to whether it governs 'Proprietary Data', 'Confidential Information' or both. This is a strong in-text contradiction because it fundamentally alters the scope and interpretation of the obligations within the section, which causes confusion and uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party shall (at the Receiving Party’s election) promptly return or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of the Receiving Party) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files) in the Receiving Party’s possession or in the possession of any Representative of the Receiving Party; provided, however: (i) that if a legal proceeding has been instituted to seek disclosure of the Confidential Information, such material shall not be destroyed until the proceeding is settled or a final judgment with respect thereto has been rendered; (ii) that the Receiving Party shall not, in connection with the foregoing obligations, be required to identify or delete Confidential Information held electronically in archive or back-up systems in accordance with general systems archiving or backup policies; and (iii) that the Receiving Party shall not be obligated to return or destroy Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to the extent the Receiving Party is required to retain a copy pursuant to applicable law, and further provided that the Receiving Party will not, and the Receiving Party will use reasonable measures to cause its employees not to, access such Confidential Information so archived or backed-up.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Data Holder shall (at the Data Holder’s election) promptly return or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of the Data Holder) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files) in the Data Holder’s possession or in the possession of any Representative of the Data Holder; provided, however: (i) that if a legal proceeding has been instituted to seek disclosure of the Confidential Information, such material shall not be destroyed until the proceeding is settled or a final judgment with respect thereto has been rendered; (ii) that the Data Holder shall not, in connection with the foregoing obligations, be required to identify or delete Confidential Information held electronically in archive or back-up systems in accordance with general systems archiving or backup policies; and (iii) that the Data Holder shall not be obligated to return or destroy Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to the extent the Data Holder is required to retain a copy pursuant to applicable law, and further provided that the Data Holder will not, and the Data Holder will use reasonable measures to cause its employees not to, access such Confidential Information so archived or backed-up.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Receiving Party” to “Data Holder” within paragraph 9, but not throughout the entire document, especially in its relation to “Confidential Information”, creates an In-Text contradiction. Since the definition of receiving party is not “Data Holder” this create ambiguity and confusion on whether this paragraph applies to the receiving party or the data holder, which might be a completely different entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Data Holder shall (at the Data Holder’s election) promptly return or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of the Data Holder) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files) in the Data Holder’s possession or in the possession of any Representative of the Data Holder; provided, however: (i) that if a legal proceeding has been instituted to seek disclosure of the Confidential Information, such material shall not be destroyed until the proceeding is settled or a final judgment with respect thereto has been rendered; (ii) that the Data Holder shall not, in connection with the foregoing obligations, be required to identify or delete Confidential Information held electronically in archive or back-up systems in accordance with general systems archiving or backup policies; and (iii) that the Data Holder shall not be obligated to return or destroy Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to the extent the Data Holder is required to retain a copy pursuant to applicable law, and further provided that the Data Holder will not, and the Data Holder will use reasonable measures to cause its employees not to, access such Confidential Information so archived or backed-up.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Data Holder' creates an In-Text contradiction because the definition of Receiving Party is not "Data Holder", creating ambiguity. The Changed Text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Data Holder', while the rest of the document may still refer to 'Receiving Party', especially in defining obligations related to 'Confidential Information.' The Contradicted Text uses 'Data Holder', implying that the obligations now specifically apply to the 'Data Holder' as opposed to the 'Receiving Party', which introduces ambiguity and confusion as to whom the paragraph applies, based on the given definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that neither the Disclosing Party nor its Representatives nor any of the officers, directors, employees, agents or controlling persons of such Representatives makes any express or implied representation or warranty regarding the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, any representation or warranty as to the completeness or accuracy of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that neither the Releasing Party nor its Representatives nor any of the officers, directors, employees, agents or controlling persons of such Representatives makes any express or implied representation or warranty regarding the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, any representation or warranty as to the completeness or accuracy of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Disclosing Party” to “Releasing Party” only in paragraph 14 without updating the definition creates contradiction within the section. The paragraph now does not align with the defined term, and create confusion on who is being referred to by the use of 'Releasing Party' , who is making the warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that neither the Releasing Party nor its Representatives nor any of the officers, directors, employees, agents or controlling persons of such Representatives makes any express or implied representation or warranty regarding the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, any representation or warranty as to the completeness or accuracy of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that changing "Disclosing Party" to "Releasing Party" only in paragraph 14 without updating the definition creates contradiction within the section and leads to confusion. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which highlights the fact that the term 'Releasing Party' is used but not defined, causing an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
